name: Georgia Construction Industry Licensed Trades Search

agency.name: Georgia Secretary of State State Construction Industry Licensing Board

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the official GOALS license search before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: Georgia master and journeyman plumber licenses
Georgia utility contractor, utility manager, and utility foreman licenses exposed by the official search
knownExclusions: Residential and commercial general contractor licenses are governed by a separate Georgia board and source entry.
Asbestos abatement contractor firms are licensed by Georgia EPD and are outside this source entry.
Local permits, business registrations, and construction specialties not regulated under Title 43 Chapter 14 are outside this source entry.
The shared GOALS search covers many professions; verification must retain the selected board and license type.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Do not bypass portal controls; prefer an official roster or export if a stable public contract is documented.
publicRecordsNotes: Official licensed-trades metadata only. Absence from this shared search is not proof that no state trade credential, local permit, or other authorization exists.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: Georgia SOS offers roster-request services, but no no-cost stable machine-readable export, schema, completeness statement, or redistribution position has been reviewed for these boards.
researchNotes: The State Construction Industry Licensing Board consists of conditioned air, electrical, low voltage, plumbing, and utility divisions. The shared GOALS lookup remains a manual handoff until its access controls and result contract are reviewed.
verificationCaveats: The board and credential type must be preserved because the shared search spans multiple licensed trades and professions.
No matching record means no match in the selected official source at the checked time, not proof that no authorization exists.
verificationNotes: No supported sync or verification adapter is exposed while the shared interactive portal lacks a reviewed stable machine interface.
maintainerNotes: Keep each board division and credential type visible in future canonical mappings. Never collapse individual and business credentials into one inferred contractor status.

researchEvidence.note: Official SOS page identifies the five State Construction Industry Licensing Board divisions and their contractor-licensing role.
Official rules page identifies statewide electrical, plumbing, conditioned-air, and low-voltage license rules under Title 43 Chapter 14.
Official shared public license-search entry point reviewed for access shape and current automation constraints.

blocker.summary: The shared JavaScript portal has no reviewed stable public data contract or export suitable for an offline-tested adapter.
